name: Tyler Cox
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Laboratory spectroscopy • Planetary materials • Mid-infrared remote sensing
bio: Christopher (Tyler) Cox is a Ph.D. student in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University, where he works as a Graduate Research Assistant in planetary remote sensing and spectroscopy. His research focuses on the characterization of planetary materials using advanced techniques such as photothermal infrared (O-PTIR) spectroscopy, contributing to the development of spectral databases for planetary science applications. Cox has authored research on mid-infrared material identification, helping improve methods for analyzing the composition of planetary surfaces such as Mars. His work integrates laboratory spectroscopy, data analysis, and planetary science to support ongoing efforts in remote sensing and space exploration.
